Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Packaging a Gleam app into a single executable 64 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. First in-utero stem cell therapy for fetal spina bifida repair is safe: study 211 upvotes, 32 comments
  3. "That Shape Had None" – A Horror of Substrate Independence (Short Fiction) 50 upvotes, 7 comments
  4. Inside the M4 Apple Neural Engine, Part 1: Reverse Engineering 218 upvotes, 55 comments
  5. Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS 1927 upvotes, 689 comments
  6. Show HN: Pianoterm – Run shell commands from your Piano. A Linux CLI tool 13 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. Show HN: uBlock filter list to blur all Instagram Reels 60 upvotes, 17 comments
  8. LFortran compiles fpm 26 upvotes, 5 comments
  9. Microsoft bans the word "Microslop" on its Discord, then locks the server 957 upvotes, 422 comments
  10. Notes on Lagrange Interpolating Polynomials 34 upvotes, 9 comments
  11. Build your own Command Line with ANSI escape codes (2016) 23 upvotes, 5 comments
  12. Making Video Games in 2025 (without an engine) 387 upvotes, 183 comments
  13. Programmable Cryptography 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. Show HN: Govbase – Follow a bill from source text to news bias to social posts 117 upvotes, 58 comments
  15. Use the Mikado Method to do safe changes in a complex codebase 127 upvotes, 64 comments
  16. British Columbia to end time changes, adopt year-round daylight time 112 upvotes, 56 comments
  17. Zclaw – The 888 KiB Assistant 67 upvotes, 35 comments
  18. Launch HN: OctaPulse (YC W26) – Robotics and computer vision for fish farming 49 upvotes, 26 comments
  19. Boss-CSS: I created another "CSS-in-JS" lib 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  20. Welcome (back) to Macintosh 28 upvotes, 14 comments
  21. Parallel coding agents with tmux and Markdown specs 90 upvotes, 67 comments
  22. Language Model Contains Personality Subnetworks 37 upvotes, 24 comments
  23. iPhone 17e 127 upvotes, 111 comments
  24. How to talk to anyone and why you should 492 upvotes, 490 comments
  25. Why Objective-C 105 upvotes, 96 comments
  26. Ask HN: Who is hiring? (March 2026) 131 upvotes, 173 comments
  27. New iPad Air, powered by M4 262 upvotes, 433 comments
  28. A case for Go as the best language for AI agents 109 upvotes, 183 comments
  29. Ask HN: Who wants to be hired? (March 2026) 42 upvotes, 121 comments
  30. Reflex (YC W23) Is Hiring Software Engineers – Python 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 6:00am, 3 March 2026. Source here.
